item.title: WA woman who poked holes in housemate's condoms found not guilty of intent to cause harm
item.description: A Bunbury woman has been found not guilty of intent to cause harm after she admitted to tampering with her housemate's condoms while she was in a "fit of rage".

item.title: Judge orders Sanjeev Gupta's Tahmoor mine be wound up with liquidators appointed
item.description: A Supreme Court judge approves a winding-up order for the embattled Tahmoor mine, owned by billionaire Sanjeev Gupta.

item.title: Adelaide man spent his final moments ramped outside hospital, partner says
item.description: An Adelaide man spent some of his final moments stuck in an ambulance outside a major Adelaide public hospital, his partner says.

item.title: Indonesia will ban social media for children under 16
item.description: Indonesia will be the first country in Southeast Asia to restrict the access of children to social media.

item.title: Donald Trump demands 'unconditional surrender' from Iran, as US and Israel vow to intensify strikes
item.description: Donald Trump says there will be no US-Israeli deal with Iran that does not involve Tehran's "unconditional surrender" just hours after Iran's president claimed mediation efforts were underway.

item.title: AUKUS yokes Australia to America's actions whether we like it or not
item.description: It seems that Australia finds itself yoked to America's actions whether the government likes it or not.

item.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: This week we're testing your knowledge of Ghostbusters, French food and classic children's books
item.description: Every question has only two options: One is right and the other is wrong. You have an even chance of guessing correctly each time.

item.title: Satellite image shows direct strikes on military buildings next to Iranian school where children were killed
item.description: ABC NEWS Verify has analysed footage and satellite imagery to investigate what happened at a girl's elementary school in Minab, Iran.

item.title: How Japan is addressing overtourism — and what it means for Australians
item.description: Travel operators say overtourism is concentrated in Japan's high-profile destinations including Tokyo, Kyoto, Osaka and areas around Mount Fuji.

item.title: Agricultural shows are losing volunteers, but new generation gives hope
item.description: More than 100 agricultural shows take place across Victoria each year and the number of volunteers is in decline, but a new generation of artisans, farmers and judges is giving shows hope for the future.

item.title: How dingo behaviour on K'gari changed in the years before Piper James's death
item.description: Piper James's tragic death is the third recorded fatality from a dingo attack in Australia. Here is a brief history of dingo management and interactions on K'gari.

item.title: Street trees boost property value in Sydney suburbs by up to 1 per cent, research finds
item.description: The location of a street tree could be the difference between a property value boost or drop, new research finds.

item.title: Cory Bernardi stands by bestiality claim ahead of SA election
item.description: One Nation's South Australian lead candidate Cory Bernardi says he "100 per cent" stands by highly controversial comments he made almost 14 years ago linking gay marriage to the social acceptance of bestiality.

item.title: Is the US dollar losing reserve currency status? The RBA has some thoughts
item.description: The US dollar's status as a safe-haven currency is questioned at a business gathering in New York.

item.title: Trump's attack on Iran could drive up Australia's power prices
item.description: Australia is a gas "superpower", but its citizens continue to pay the price through their electricity bills for the country failing to protect them from the fallout as the Middle East unravels again.

item.title: North West Island on the Great Barrier Reef declared rat-free
item.description: The invasive pests were attacking nests of vulnerable seabirds and turtles on a Great Barrier Reef island.

item.title: Nightcliff by-election: Greens bid to hold Darwin seat challenged by three contenders
item.description: It started as a historic win for the Greens, but now the minor party is in for a fight to preserve its solitary seat in the Northern Territory Parliament.

item.title: 34 people rescued from bus in floodwaters as much of Qld on flood watch
item.description: A tropical low moving through northern parts of Queensland and tracking west is dragging persistent wet weather across the region.

item.title: In Daintree village, the river peaked at 8.85 meters early Friday morning
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Man killed at Mernda train station was good Samaritan protecting teenage boy, police say
item.description: Police say the 22-year-old man was stabbed to death while trying to protect a 14-year-old boy from a group attack at a train station in Melbourne's outer north.

item.title: Australia's Annabel Sutherland dominates against India on day one of pink-ball Test
item.description: Teammates and rivals praise all-rounder Annabel Sutherland after her starring role with the ball on day one of the pink-ball Test between Australia and India.

item.title: Under pressure on hate speech laws, the Queensland government seeks to divert attention
item.description: The government made a flurry of other policy announcements to distract from its 11th-hour changes to its hate speech laws, writes state political reporter Alex Brewster.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: How an unfancied, injury-hit squad fired the Matildas to their greatest triumph
item.description: Former coach Tom Sermanni says "it wasn't his greatest look". But for its part in helping the Matildas to 2010 Asian Cup glory, it may well be the most consequential makeover in Australian football history.

item.title: Ongoing dry conditions cause ant infestations across South Australia's Riverland
item.description: Prolonged periods of drought have caused swarms of ants to infest homes across South Australia's Riverland

item.title: Decision on sale of Tasmania's largest farm Rushy Lagoon expected soon
item.description: The 22,000-hectare Rushy Lagoon property has long been used for dairy and beef production, but its potential sale to the UK's largest forestry asset manager has raised concerns about agricultural land being used for pine plantations.

item.title: Melissa Lucashenko shares about genesis of new book Not Quite White in the Head
item.description: Melissa Lucashenko is one of Australia's most-acclaimed authors. But when it became clear to the Miles Franklin Literary Award winner that a self-professed fan had only read her non-fiction work, she decided it was time to put together a collection of essays called Not Quite White in the Head.

item.title: NSW government ends 400km border fence project seven years in the making
item.description: Bourke Shire Mayor Lachlan Ford says finding out that a major extension of a wild dog barrier had been cancelled without consultation added insult to injury.

item.title: Katherine flooding nears peak as 'major flood' risk to remain until Sunday
item.description: Floodwater is entering parts of Katherine's town centre and overwhelming the town's levee system. The water level is expected to peak at 19.2 metres tonight, and will remain a "major flood risk" until Sunday evening.

item.title: 'More pain to come' as oil faces biggest weekly price surge since 1983
item.description: US crude oil has posted its biggest weekly gain in futures trading history, with the war in the Middle East leading to a major disruption to global fuel supplies.

item.title: Concerns over 'escalating unrest' at regional hospital after senior doctor escorted from building
item.description: Community outrage flares as a "respected and trusted" senior doctor is escorted from a regional hospital amid the fall-out from a controversial redevelopment project.

item.title: How one week of war has changed Iran
item.description: While the country's strict Islamist regime has blocked the internet and other forms of communication for most people, some have found a way to get their stories out.

item.title: How Constellations rose from the ashes of the cancelled Adelaide Writers' Week
item.description: Constellations emerged from the ashes of Adelaide Writers' Week, which was cancelled after authors withdrew over censorship. Organisers share how it came together and lessons for the future.

item.title: Calls for a national ethanol mandate to prevent future Iran war-style oil shocks
item.description: Only two states make it a requirement to blend ethanol into fuel, but Australia's sugar industry says making it a national requirement could shield motorists from future oil market shocks.

item.title: Obeid family loses control of $30m worth of Sydney properties after one of NSW's 'most brazen acts of corruption'
item.description: After investigations spanning a decade, the NSW Crime Commission says it has taken control of more than $30 million worth of properties linked to former New South Wales Labor politician Eddie Obeid.

item.title: IDF visualisation of Ayatollah’s bunker in Tehran
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Anne Aly warns Australia must not 'abandon' people vulnerable to radicalisation
item.description: Radicalisation expert turned federal minister Anne Aly says reaching out to people susceptible to extremism and helping them reconnect to their communities is essential.

item.title: PNG cannot safely store Australian-supplied rifles, military boss warns
item.description: The head of Papua New Guinea's military says the country does not have the capacity to safely store 3,500 rifles Australia plans to ship to the country, raising fears they could end up in the hands of tribal warlords.

item.title: The 'Cranky Women's Club' using comedy to tackle serious issues
item.description: A funny thing happened on the way to menopause. Twelve first-time comedians are finding the humour in some of their most confronting life experiences.

item.title: WA shires charged 'ridiculous' traffic management costs for events, maintenance work
item.description: A local government in WA's South West calls for Main Roads to review the cost of traffic management after the council had to stop maintenance works due to the expense.

item.title: Embrace simplicity with baked eggplant and soy-garlic chicken mince - ABC News
item.description: This dish is reminiscent of moussaka but it's a more pared-back meal that's not as rich and involves fewer steps.

item.title: Basil Zempilas tells conservative conference Liberals must re-emerge as political force
item.description: WA Opposition Leader Basil Zempilas tells a conservative conference the Liberal Party must stop haemorrhaging voters and win back "lost Australians".

item.title: South Sydney coach Wayne Bennett rules Rabbitohs out of Zac Lomax pursuit
item.description: Wayne Bennett rules South Sydney out of the Zac Lomax stakes, with the Rabbitohs coach insisting the exiled State of Origin star must live with the consequences of his split with Parramatta.

item.title: Remote NT communities of Daly River, Palumpa evacuated as flood threat continues
item.description: Hundreds of remote residents are being evacuated from their small communities by air this weekend, as flooding inundates parts of the Northern Territory.

item.title: 'Mini supreme leader' Mojtaba Khamenei could soon rise but he's already the 'power behind the robes'
item.description: As senior clerics responsible for selecting Iran's next supreme leader met this week to discuss their pick, a familiar name emerged as the frontrunner: Mojtaba Khamenei.

item.title: MAGA supporters speak on Trump's Middle East war
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Wet weather triggers flooding in Palumpa
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Lions claim 'alarm bells' aren’t ringing after loss to Bulldogs
item.description: The Brisbane Lions are refusing to panic after their premiership defence began on a losing note to the Western Bulldogs at the Gabba.

item.title: Superstar Sutherland backs up record-breaking ton with key wickets
item.description: An all-round masterclass by Annabel Sutherland has Australia on the brink of victory against India after day two of the day-night Test at the WACA.

item.title: Brisbane media personality, broadcaster, and Agro puppeteer Jamie Dunn dies aged 76
item.description: Dunn was a long-standing host of the B105 breakfast program but was known across the country as Agro — a puppet made out of a bath mat that rose to fame in 1990s.

item.title: 'Weren't planning on doing it': How Raiders pulled off match-winning play
item.description: Ethan Sanders reveals what went through his mind before he kicked a field goal to take the Raiders to a thrilling 29-28 victory over Manly in golden point.

item.title: Austin Appelbee reunited with rescuers who saved his family in WA's South West
item.description: A WA family who made global headlines when they were rescued after being swept out to sea have been reunited with the local heroes who saved them.

item.title: Kyle and Jackie O superfans devastated they're not seeing eye-to-eye
item.description: An actress, electrician and real estate agent all have one thing in common – their love for Kyle and Jackie O. Here's why they've stuck by the pair through thick and thin.

item.title: One Nation selects agricultural businessman David Farley to contest Farrer by-election in NSW
item.description: David Farley, 69, defeats two other candidates in today's preselection battle at the Albury Entertainment Centre.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: 'No way' Iran’s footballers can return home, with calls to protect them in Australia
item.description: The Iranian women's football team is facing an impossible situation. Return home, and possibly face punishment from the Islamic Republic regime for its silent protest, or stay in Australia and their families could instead suffer retribution.

item.title: Labor on track to win Nightcliff by-election, taking NT's single Greens seat
item.description: Labor is on track to win the Nightcliff by-election, taking the Greens' first and only seat held in the Northern Territory parliament.

item.title: Aftermath of Israeli strikes on Hezbollah-controlled Beirut suburbs
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: 'Unexpected' deaths in aged care are rising. One brother demanded to see the coroner
item.description: Attila's shaky phone video of his brother lying dead in aged care documented bruises and blood. It was the start of a long campaign to understand what happened.

item.title: Australia plays an uncomfortable and important role as a middle power
item.description: Middle powers may have a larger role to play in the emerging world order, but the same old dilemma persists: how far are they willing to defend the rules when the countries bending them are their closest allies?

item.title: Hard Quiz: You can insist you're the best at trivia but I know the truth
item.description: I'm so glad you're back for another round! Just kidding, as if I'd ever say that. Let's play, HARD!

item.title: The Farrer by-election is a test for the Coalition. For voters it's an opportunity
item.description: The upcoming Farrer by-election is already being talked about as a litmus test for the new opposition leader and an ascendant One Nation. It'll also be a measure of major party politics.

item.title: Five works not to miss at Iconic Loved Unexpected at Newcastle Art Gallery
item.description: The newly expanded gallery reopens with a blockbuster exhibition, Iconic Loved Unexpected, that places local artists alongside international stars.

item.title: Iran strikes are Donald Trump's ninth foreign military action in 14 months
item.description: The Australian government has avoided stating whether it supports or opposes many of Trump's operations.

item.title: SA government attributed email to wrong man after his death, traumatising family
item.description: A grieving woman "feels sick" after the government releases an email it claims was written by her deceased partner praising his hospital care, after she said ambulance ramping had denied him dignity in his final hours.

item.title: Looking for healing after the Bondi massacre
item.description: Michael Visontay explores the deep roots of Jewish life in Bondi, and the impact of loss on this close-knit community.

item.title: Consensus reached on Iran's next leader, local media reports — as it happened
item.description: A majority consensus on who will be the successor to Ayatollah Ali Khamenei has been reached, according to members of the Assembly of Experts.

item.title: Donald Trump threatens to hit Iran 'very hard' as Tehran clerics vow to pick new leader
item.description: It came as Iran's President Masoud Pezeshkian dismissed Mr Trump's demand for the country's surrender as a "dream" and apologised to Gulf states for missile and drone attacks.

item.title: All-ages heavy metal music events in Sydney not dead as young people keep headbanging
item.description: In community halls across Western Sydney young people are headbanging, crowdsurfing and forming mosh pits.

item.title: SA election promises ramped down as ambulance crisis proves hard to solve
item.description: With just two weeks to go until the state election, Labor has steered away from ambitious promises after difficulty meeting its pledge to fix the ambulance ramping crisis last election — one of the few points where the opposition's barbs are piercing.

item.title: Glenthompson community keeps town pool despite council's closure vote
item.description: While council pools across Victoria face threats of closure, residents of a rural town beat the odds to save theirs. Now they have to prove a community-run model can work.

item.title: Australia hoping Alyssa Healy bats one last time in Test farewell against India in Perth
item.description: Alyssa Healy is being encouraged to promote herself up the batting order, as the Australia captain prepares for the final act of her international career.

item.title: Two teens, man charged with murdering good Samaritan Aidan Becker in Mernda
item.description: Two teenagers and a man have been charged after 22-year-old Aidan Becker died from stab wounds in Mernda on Friday night. Police are waiting to interview a fourth person about the death.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Matildas hope other nations don't have to keep fighting for the basics
item.description: There was a time when the Matildas had to fight and scrap for pay, recognition, and respect. They hope the Women's Asian Cup can change things for other nations.

item.title: Russia hits Ukraine with drones, missiles, killing at least 10 in Kharkiv
item.description: Two children are among at least 10 people dead after a Russian missile hit a five-story residential building in Ukraine's second-largest city, Kharkiv.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: What time do the Matildas play? How to watch and follow Australia vs South Korea
item.description: The Matildas must win their final Women's Asian Cup group game against South Korea in Sydney tonight to top their group and earn an easier passage through the knockout stage.

item.title: Female farmers feel overlooked, under-represented due to 'patchy progress'
item.description: After 30 years of "patchy progress", women in agriculture are striving for more recognition and understanding of their crucial role on the land.

item.title: Katherine experiences worst flooding in 28 years as remote NT communities evacuated
item.description: Katherine experiences its worst flooding in 28 years, with several Territorians rescued by helicopter this weekend after they were stranded in a nearby remote community.

item.title: Women return to Mackay surfboat racing after 30-year absence
item.description: Thirty years after the death of a beloved member during training, Mackay Surf Lifesaving Club revives its women's surfboat team.

item.title: Mackay SLSC Surf Boat Rowing
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: City of Hobart shipwreck officially found off Gippsland after 150 years
item.description: A renewable energy company's search for an offshore wind farm site leads divers to the wreckage of the City of Hobart, which sank nearly 150 years ago off Victoria's east coast.

item.title: Celebrated for its sea life and pristine beaches, Shark Bay is preserved by those who love it
item.description: This tiny community is enduring the ultimate balancing act, keeping hordes of tourists happy, mitigating the impacts of climate change and protecting their World Heritage-listed home. Lisa Millar explores how Shark Bay not only survives, but thrives.

item.title: Government considering request for military assistance from Gulf States attacked by Iran
item.description: Foreign Affairs Minister Penny Wong says the government is considering requests for military assistance from Gulf countries targeted by Iranian attacks.

item.title: Ian Huntley, one of UK's most notorious child killers, dies after prison attack
item.description: Ian Huntley had been on life support after being hit over the head with a metal bar at Frankland prison in north-east England.

item.title: Free dance classes run by 12yo champ keep girls on their toes in Armidale
item.description: Financial stress means some families cannot afford their children's dance classes, so Armidale tween Annabelle has started a free dance school to keep their dreams alive.

item.title: The K-pop concert that sparked an online war across Asia
item.description: Criticism of Korean fans bringing professional cameras into a Korean boy band concert in Malaysia, against local rules, spiralled into an online war from Vietnam to Indonesia.

item.title: Dolphins and a seal found dead on Eyre Peninsula beaches
item.description: Large, dead marine animals found on Eyre Peninsula beaches spark concern among locals.

item.title: Menopause isn't easy, but for women living in Pilbara heat becomes 'stress multiplier'
item.description: Pilbara woman Bree Maher spent nearly a year trying to find the right support for her menopause symptoms. She said the heat of northern Western Australia amplified her feelings of isolation.

item.title: How to improve the energy efficiency in older Australian homes
item.description: Owners of older homes are being urged to have their energy efficiency assessed, with experts saying improvements will not only make them more comfortable, but help them save money as well.

item.title: Cane toads bred in WA's Kimberley protect goannas from fatal consequences
item.description: Field work is underway near Broome where researchers are deliberately breeding the invasive cane toad as part of a strategy to protect native wildlife.

item.title: Labor leader Josh Willie says public sector cuts won't solve state's budget crisis
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Tumut is hoping to become the giant pumpkin capital of Australia
item.description: Deep in the NSW Snowy Mountains, these heavyweights of the veggie patch are determined to grow the biggest pumpkins in the country.

item.title: Fire billows from oil depot in Tehran
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Demonstrators rally in Washington DC to show support for Iran operation
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Fire breaks out at Tehran oil facility after reported air strike
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Are couples happier sleeping separately?
item.description: Despite many people like Hannah finding a "sleep divorce" improves their sleep — and their relationship — a "pervasive stigma" about not sharing a bed persists.

item.title: How some airlines are still flying between Australia, Asia and Europe
item.description: Travel routes between Australia, Asia and Europe are narrowing. Here is how airlines are still flying the Kangaroo Route despite wars in the Middle East and Ukraine.

item.title: Blazes at oil facilities light up Tehran’s night sky
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Crocodile crossing the road during NT flood event
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: SA government's cancer patient email blunder was 'completely unacceptable', premier says
item.description: Peter Malinauskas says his government made an "unacceptable" error in releasing an email it claimed was from a terminal cancer patient who later died, but that turned out to be from a different man with the same name.

item.title: High rise in Kuwait City blazes after Iranian drone strike
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Australia and Canada share similar values. Remind us what they are again
item.description: When Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney visited Australia last week, he had a message about Australia's and Canada's shared values. He said they were worth defending and could be used to build a more prosperous future.

item.title: Bennett pleads with fans to stay off the field after Johnston's record try
item.description: The Rabbitohs take down the Dolphins in a 70-point clash at Lang Park, with South Sydney's Alex Johnston scoring his 211th career try to move within one of Ken Irvine's record.

item.title: Healy bids farewell as Australia seals crushing Test win over India
item.description: Alyssa Healy signs off her illustrious career in style as Australia wipes away India to claim an easy win in Perth.

item.title: Western Sydney earmarked as potential location for state's second film studio
item.description: The NSW government has earmarked three areas in Western Sydney as possible locations for the state's second film studio.

item.title: Nationals select former military commander Brad Robertson to contest Farrer by-election
item.description: The National Party's candidate for the Farrer by-election, Brad Robertson, says the people of Farrer have been "waiting" for the Nationals to come back.

item.title: Premier's boat trip passed into sanctuary zone while towing lures, RecFish SA concedes
item.description: An election campaign boat trip involving the SA premier has faced criticism after the state's peak recreational fishing body — which says it organised the trip — admitted it breached marine regulations by towing lures in a sanctuary zone.

item.title: Collingwood claims spiteful MCG thriller against St Kilda
item.description: St Kilda's star recruits aren't enough to overcome Collingwood in a thriller at the MCG to cap off Opening Round.

item.title: Matildas finish second in Asian Cup group after pulsating draw with South Korea
item.description: Alanna Kennedy nets a last-gasp equaliser, but it's not enough for the Matildas. They finish second in Group A after a pulsating 3-3 draw with Korea Republic in Sydney.

item.title: Floodwaters flowing down Katherine Gorge quickly
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: When will schools and shops reopen? How Katherine and surrounds will move through flood recovery
item.description: Wondering if schools in the Top End will be open tomorrow? Not sure what the situation is with flood evacuation centres? Looking for the latest information on natural disaster relief payments? We've answered all your NT flood-related questions here.

item.title: F1 Australian Grand Prix quick hits: Oscar Piastri silences the crowd, George Russell predicts Ferrari's 'rocket' launch
item.description: Australia's collective hearts sank as Piastri went into the wall, while race winner George Russell pulled off a prediction that Nostradamus would have been proud of. These are the key moments from qualifying at Albert Park.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Steph Catley floored by head shot and Ellie Carpenter rages at officials — Matildas vs South Korea quick hits
item.description: Steph Catley gets floored and Ellie Carpenter rages as Alanna Kennedy continues her hot streak. Here are the quick hits from the Matildas' 3-3 draw against Korea.

item.title: Labor acutely aware of risks in backing US-Israeli attacks on Iran
item.description: The Albanese government will need to be explicit about what it is doing in the Middle East or risk a growing concern that it is being drawn into another war.

item.title: Oil markets can't bank on deal maker Donald Trump in the Middle East
item.description: Investors seem calm, relying on Donald Trump's reputation as a deal maker but the reality is this conflict could be long and destabilising.

item.title: New tourist tax for popular Twelve Apostles on Victoria's Great Ocean Road
item.description: The amount tourists will be charged to visit the Twelve Apostles is subject to consultation with stakeholders.

item.title: Australian crime syndicates using fake tobacco packaging to flood legal market
item.description: Australian crime syndicates are using fake plain packaging — mass produced overseas — to legitimise illicit tobacco and flood the legal market with knock-offs.

item.title: Public housing tenant blocked from own backyard, as disability advocates say accessibility an 'afterthought'
item.description: For more than two years, Sharryn Johns has been unable to access her own backyard. It took questions from the ABC for the public housing authority to take action.

item.title: What is a career break and how do you ask for one?
item.description: Whether it’s for a holiday, additional study or a exploring a new field of work, experts say a career break can have many benefits.

item.title: This week in history quiz, March 9: Can you pass the test?
item.description: A royal family photo, Buffy the Vampire Slayer and Australia's first inland city.

item.title: Courtney Moir's Muster Dogs win fuels passion for dog handling, motherhood
item.description: After swapping corporate city life for her family's remote seventh-generation farm, Courtney Moir has claimed the top spot in this year's season of Muster Dogs.

item.title: Dubai-based Australian companies caught in crossfire as tensions escalate in Middle East
item.description: Escalating geopolitical tensions mean Australians operating businesses in the Middle East have had to make quick changes to their daily operations.

item.title: Russia a 'big winner' in oil crisis sparked by Middle East war and Strait of Hormuz closure
item.description: Russia has condemned the US and Israeli strikes on Iran, but its economy and in turn war efforts could be boosted thanks the oil crisis created from the war.

item.title: What will happen to my son when I die?
item.description: Deirdre Croft has dedicated 45 years to caring and advocating for her intellectually disabled son, Richard. Each passing day brings more pressure to secure his future when she's gone. It's a distressing scenario thousands of Australian parents face.

item.title: Israel expands Iran strikes as Tehran moves to name new supreme leader
item.description: Israeli forces expand their bombardment of Iran, striking fuel depots near Tehran as the country moves closer to naming a new supreme leader.

item.title: What you need to know about Iran today, with Laura Tingle
item.description: The ABC's global affairs editor, Laura Tingle, is in Dubai and will provide daily updates on the Middle East war.

item.title: Defiant and proud — Australian Jews speak out in first ABC National Forum
item.description: Along with authors, teachers, students, parents and grandparents, Kahn will tell her story of life in Australia as a Jew in the first ABC National Forum.

item.title: Live: Trump says ending war will be 'mutual' decision with Israeli PM as strikes heard across region
item.description: US President Donald Trump says he has been talking with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, who will have input on when to end the war with Iran. Follow live.

item.title: EMDR mental health therapy helped Katie heal, but her court case was discontinued
item.description: Katie Bird sought psychological help for intrusive memories related to alleged sexual abuse. Her court case was discontinued because she underwent eye movement desensitisation and reprocessing (EMDR) therapy.

item.title: South Australian voters want more transport options, such as Adelaide Hills rail
item.description: As more houses are being built in Adelaide's outer suburbs, more public transport is something on the minds of South Australian voters, in both the regions and the city.

item.title: Australia sitting on over $1 billion of unused medical research funding as scientists abandon the field
item.description: Researchers say securing funding has become increasingly tough, with many left with no choice but to spend a disproportionate amount of time applying for grants.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Frustrated Matildas exposed by South Korea, but say long road won't derail them
item.description: In the moments that mattered, the Matildas lacked composure, while South Korea made them pay. They'll now have to do it the hard way if they're going to go all the way at the Women's Asian Cup.

item.title: ASX sheds $90 billion as oil's 25pc surge crushes Asian markets
item.description: The All Ordinaries has shed 2.9pc, or about $90 billion in value, as global markets crumble and oil prices surge 25pc above $US110/barrel and global interest rates start to climb on inflation fears.

item.title: Koolewong community rallies to help bushfire survivors
item.description: Paula Jones and Ringo Hansen feared they would be homeless after last year's bushfires, but a NSW Central Coast community ensured that did not happen.

item.title: Two injured in alleged machete attack in Melbourne CBD, days after fatal stabbing in city's north
item.description: The alleged attack took place during the busy Formula 1 and Moomba long weekend, and just days after a fatal stabbing in the city's north.

item.title: Government won't say which Gulf state requested Australia's military assistance — as it happened
item.description: The government is currently considering a request to provide military assistance to a Gulf nation, but it won't say which one. Take a look back at the day's coverage.

item.title: Major flood warning issued for Bundaberg with BOM warning 2010 levels possible
item.description: Widespread major flooding is possible for the Queensland city of Bundaberg as heavy rainfall is forecast for already soaked catchments.

item.title: How the internet reacted to the Matildas' 3-3 draw with South Korea
item.description: The Matildas played out a ding-dong 3-3 draw with Korea Republic in Sydney; the result wasn't enough for Australia to top the group, but the internet was enthralled by the goal-fest.

item.title: Paramedic Harriet Moore honoured for heroic care during fatal ambulance crash
item.description: A NSW South Coast paramedic is recognised for her commitment after continuing to care for patients after sustaining injuries in a fatal crash.

item.title: Apocalyptic scenes in aftermath of US-Israel strikes on Iranian oil depots
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: India dominates New Zealand in the T20 World Cup final to claim back-to-back titles
item.description: India is sent in to bat in the T20 World Cup final and dominates from start to finish to claim its third title.

item.title: Cash-strapped neighbourhood houses seek extra funding amid cost-of-living strain
item.description: More people are turning to Victoria's neighbourhood houses for support as cost-of-living pressures bite. The neighbourhood houses say they can't cope with the extra demand and need state government support.

item.title: Federal funding pulled from WA charity helping people struggling with cost of living, leaving futures uncertain
item.description: As a Perth charity that provides food and financial assistance for people who are struggling loses its federal funding, there are fears it will have to turn more vulnerable people away.

item.title: Ben Tudhope wins snowboard cross silver for Australia at the Winter Paralympics
item.description: Ben Tudhope wins silver in the snowboard cross to register Australia's first medal at the 2026 Winter Paralympics.

item.title: Regional patient 'effectively ramped' for almost three days waiting for care
item.description: When Riverland farmer Mick Evans had a suspected heart attack last year, he was stranded at a regional hospital for almost three days after an airlift request was continually cancelled and there were no available beds at the Royal Adelaide Hospital.

item.title: Protesters surround Iran team bus on Gold Coast after Asian Cup match against the Philippines
item.description: Protesters surround the Iranian women's football team bus after their final match in the Asian Cup, as fears grow for their safety.

item.title: Great Western Highway shut in both directions due to defect at Victoria Pass bridge
item.description: The Great Western Highway between Lithgow and Mount Victoria is closed due to damage discovered on a historical bridge.

item.title: New F1 regulations debut at Australian Grand Prix, with mixed reviews from the paddock
item.description: Formula 1's new era began with a dramatic, chaotic, and compelling grand prix in Melbourne. While views on the regulations are mixed, fans will likely be the ones to decide if it is a success or failure.

item.title: Campers flooded in at Byfield following heavy rains
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Phosphate Hill fertiliser plant sold to Mayfair, ensuring continued operations at copper smelter
item.description: A major outback mining asset near Mount Isa has been sold to a Queensland-based company for up to $100 million, potentially securing the town's copper production.

item.title: Are the US-Israeli strikes on Iran legal?
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Why Bridget Jones's Diary still resonates with readers 30 years later
item.description: When Oxford-educated Helen Fielding introduced readers to Bridget Jones in her newspaper column, in her own words, she was "quite embarrassed". Three decades later, the book has sold more than 15 million copies worldwide.

item.title: Australia almost defeats Japan at World Baseball Classic as showdown with South Korea looms
item.description: Australia pushes baseball juggernauts Japan all the way in their World Baseball Classic clash.

item.title: Police speaking with 'a number of witnesses' following alleged campsite stabbing near Tamworth
item.description: Police allege an altercation between two men has ended in a fatal stabbing at a campsite near Tamworth.

item.title: Socceroos keeper Joe Gauci stars as Port Vale downs Sunderland in FA Cup boilover
item.description: Socceroos keeper Joe Gauci and Aussie manager Jon Brady guide Port Vale past Sunderland into the FA Cup quarters for the first time in 72 years.

item.title: Fire at Glasgow Central Station causes evacuations, train disruptions
item.description: A huge and devastating fire near Britain's Glasgow Central Station causes the cancellation of dozens of train services.

item.title: Food supply lines open as Katherine and Daly River under emergency flood warning
item.description: While Katherine remains under an emergency flood warning, water levels are now receding and food supply lines are opening up.

item.title: Who is Iran's new supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei?
item.description: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's son Mojtaba, a hardline, anti-Western mid-ranking cleric, has been chosen to succeed his late father as Iran's supreme leader.

item.title: How Donald Trump and Pete Hegseth are selling the Iran war to Americans
item.description: The US administration's unapologetic stance on the war has come with a lot of bravado, but there are signs the American public is not convinced.

item.title: Zac Lomax signs two-year Super Rugby deal in major blow to NRL
item.description: Zac Lomax signs a deal with Western Force and Rugby Australia in a major blow to the NRL.

item.title: SA health minister 'very sorry' for leak to media of wrong patient's email
item.description: South Australia's health minister apologises "unreservedly" for the deliberate leak of an email that the government said was from an Adelaide cancer patient who died in 2025, but turned out to be from a different person with the same name.

item.title: Supporters rally around Iranian women’s football team bus after Asian Cup exit
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Tasmania flags new laws to stop 'good character' evidence as mitigating factor for serious offending
item.description: Tasmania will introduce new laws to stop the use of "good character" references by serious offenders. Advocates say the state has joined a broader national shift to support victim-survivors.

item.title: Macedonian dancers pull out of Moomba parade after flag ban
item.description: The Federation of Macedonian Cultural and Artistic Associations of Victoria says it was a difficult decision but participating without the flag would compromise a key part of their identity.

item.title: Ukraine's war through the letters of its children
item.description: Ukraine's children have grown up amid four years of Vladimir Putin's war. Some who survived have revealed their sadness, hope, torment and aspirations.

item.title: Masked German football fan unplugs VAR monitor in protest during Bundesliga 2 game
item.description: Frustrated football fans take their anger at VAR to the next level, with a masked supporter pulling the plug on a replay monitor during a Bundesliga 2 game.

item.title: Desperation for Iran's women's footballers, as coach says 'we are eager to come back to Iran'
item.description: There is an increasing sense of urgency among members of the Iranian Australian community who are desperate to hear from the women's team before they leave Australia, and fear for their safety if they return to Iran.

item.title: Bluff Knoll climbers winched by rescue helicopter after being injured in fall
item.description: Two climbers injured in a fall on Bluff Knoll, in WA's Great Southern, are winched off the mountain by helicopter and taken to Albany for treatment.

item.title: Former NSW Liberal MP Rory Amon cleared of majority of charges in sexual assault trial
item.description: A jury has cleared former NSW Liberal MP Rory Amon of the majority of charges he faced over the alleged sexual assault of a 13-year-old boy he met through a hook-up app in 2017.

item.title: Prince Harry and Meghan Markle confirm Australian visit in April
item.description: The visit will mark seven years since they last touched down in the country for their successful tour as the newlywed Duke and Duchess of Sussex.

item.title: Essendon coach Brad Scott says his club has moved on from Zach Merrett saga as Bombers set to face Hawthorn
item.description: Former Essendon captain Zach Merrett is the centre of attention in the build-up to a round-one blockbuster against the club he attempted to join in the off-season.

item.title: Bywong man who dragged conscious kangaroo behind car fails bid to reduce sentence
item.description: A Bywong man's attempt to have his sentence for aggravated animal cruelty reduced, after dragging a conscious kangaroo behind his car last year, is dismissed in the Queanbeyan Local Court.

item.title: Cleaning job offer led to woman's arrest at Calderwood cannabis farm, court hears
item.description: A judge hears a 32-year-old Vietnamese woman arrested at a cannabis farm south of Wollongong had travelled to the property on the promise of a lucrative cleaning job.

item.title: Five recipes to make lunchtime leftovers a breeze
item.description: Looking to make lunchtime super simple and satisfying? Try these five dishes.

item.title: Territory Labor's Ed Smelt wins Nightcliff by-election, taking the Greens' only NT seat
item.description: A check count conducted by the Northern Territory Electoral Commission has slightly increased Ed Smelt's lead to 158 votes for Labor, over the Greens' Suki Dorras-Walker.

item.title: Lebanon mass displacement causes anxiety for diaspora in Australia
item.description: The Lebanese government says more than 500,000 people have been displaced since the Israeli military issued evacuation warnings for villages in the country's south.

item.title: Defying Donald Trump and stepping out of the shadows, Mojtaba Khamenei ascends
item.description: US President Donald Trump said the worst-case scenario in Iran would be if Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's successor were "as bad as the previous person". In many ways, the appointment of his son, Mojtaba Khamenei, delivers him that very outcome.

item.title: Scooter crash leaves Central Coast mum fighting for life in Bali hospital
item.description: A mother of two from the NSW Central Coast was holidaying in Bali with her girlfriends when she was hit by a truck while riding a scooter.

item.title: Bondi Beach gunman Naveed Akram seeks order to suppress family's identity over safety concerns
item.description: Naveed Akram faces 59 charges, including 15 counts of murder and one of committing a terrorist attack, over the shooting on December 14.

item.title: Premier Roger Cook defends moving house from Kwinana electorate to West Perth
item.description: Premier Roger Cook says his decision to move with his partner to a residence close to Parliament House will help him further focus on his work.

item.title: Cemeteries desecrated by suspected metal thieves at Quorn and Wilmington
item.description: An estimated 100 memorial plaques have been forcibly removed from two South Australian regional cemeteries, causing significant distress to loved ones.

item.title: Saltwater crocodile found in dam hundreds of kilometres from normal habitat
item.description: Authorities say they will rehome a 2.3 metre saltwater crocodile that was found in a dam near Gatton, hundreds of kilometres from its usual habitat.

item.title: NAB warns rising petrol prices could drive inflation above 5 per cent
item.description: More pain could be coming from motorists if economists' predictions about the impact of rising fuel prices on inflation prove correct.

item.title: Farmers stockpile diesel as Middle East conflict prompts fuel supply fears
item.description: Farmers say consumers could soon feel the impact of fuel shortages and soaring prices at the checkout, as the closure of the Strait of Hormuz disrupts global supply chains.

item.title: As the Iran war rages on, historic oil price surge sends share markets into a spin
item.description: For more than a year, traders have been pushing markets higher on the hopes of an AI-powered economic miracle and the notion of TACO — Trump Always Chickens Out. Now it looks like the penny has dropped.

item.title: Man charged with murder after alleged assault and robbery in Perth's CBD
item.description: Police charge a 37-year-old man with murder after he allegedly attacked a 62-year-old man in Perth's CBD, causing him to fall and strike his head on the pavement.

item.title: Fire on the streets of Tehran after explosions at oil depot
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Big Red Bash site will not be dry enough to hold festival in July, search for new venue
item.description: The event is still four months away, but organisers say the site will not be sufficiently dry in time.

item.title: Regional WA teacher pleads not guilty to assault charges against students
item.description: A female teacher is facing five common assault charges against several students.

item.title: Efforts to topple Iran's leadership may backfire and strengthen the regime, former US diplomat says
item.description: A former top US diplomat says her country's efforts to topple Iran's leadership risks destabilising the region and strengthening the nation's Revolutionary Guard.

item.title: Australian men sentenced in Indonesian court for Bali murder of Zivan Radmanovic
item.description: An Indonesian court has jailed two Australian men for 16 years for the murder of Zivan Radmanovic, 32, in June last year.

item.title: Age verification for R-rated games and websites raises privacy concerns
item.description: New online safety codes requiring R-rated games and websites to verify their users' ages come into effect, prompting concerns about online privacy.

item.title: Monday Finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: Court rules WA Housing Authority does not owe consumer guarantee to tenants
item.description: Perth Magistrates Court has ruled against a First Nations woman's challenge after a long legal battle over housing neglect in WA's Pilbara region.

item.title: Fire at Glasgow central station causes major disruption
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: ABC National Forum explores lives and experiences of Jewish Australians
item.description: ABC National Forum is a new discussion program bringing together ordinary citizens, community leaders, experts and policymakers to talk about topical issues of national significance, moderated by David Speers.

item.title: Two Jewish Australians describe their experiences of antisemitism
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Katherine locals survey damage from major flooding as volunteer 'army' rallies to help
item.description: An "army" of volunteers has hit the streets of Katherine to help residents worst affected by the weekend's flooding, as they survey the damage from near-record river rises.

item.title: Emergency services prioritised as some regional petrol stations ration fuel
item.description: Petrol stations in some WA towns begin restricting petrol sales to emergency services only, as impacts from the Iran war hit the state.

item.title: What is antisemitism?
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Labor MP Josh Burns on wearing his Star of David necklace
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: 'Bondi was not a surprise to me': School principal Jeremy Stowe-Lindner
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.
